title: Trump Pulled in at Least $2.2 Billion in 2025, Financial Disclosure Shows
summary: The release of a mandatory financial disclosure for 2025 shows that the Trump family’s holdings, particularly the president’s crypto businesses, were stunningly lucrative.

title: Trump’s Moneymaking Run: Unrivaled in Presidential History
summary: The president’s move to open new business ventures, rather than eliminate potential conflicts, defies a long-held tradition.

title: Supreme Court Blocks Trump’s Push to End Birthright Citizenship
summary: Also, a House Republican says depression led to his long absence. Here's the latest at the end of Tuesday.

title: Supreme Court Upholds Birthright Citizenship, Blocking a Key Trump Policy
summary: The justices blocked President Trump’s executive order that banned birthright citizenship for the children of undocumented immigrants and some temporary foreign visitors.

title: What the Supreme Court Decisions About Trump’s Firings Mean
summary: Our chief legal affairs correspondent, Adam Liptak, explains how two Supreme Court rulings on the firings of independent regulators first expand the power of the president, and then carve out an exception.

title: Supreme Court Lifts Spending Limits on Political Parties and Candidates
summary: Republicans had asked the court to strike down restrictions on how much political parties can spend in coordination with candidates.

title: Supreme Court Allows States to Bar Transgender Athletes From Girls’ Sports
summary: The court’s decision involving laws from West Virginia and Idaho has implications for 25 other states with similar restrictions on transgender female athletes joining women’s sports teams.

title: Takeaways From a Transformative Supreme Court Term
summary: The justices rejected some of President Trump’s signature initiatives, but delivered lasting, long-sought conservative wins.

title: U.S. Lifts Restrictions on Anthropic’s Most Powerful A.I. Models
summary: The move allows Anthropic to bring its most powerful technologies back online, de-escalating a feud with the Trump administration.

title: G.O.P. Revolt Paralyzes the House, Stalling Trump Agenda
summary: A Republican blockade derailed a Pentagon policy bill and other legislation as the far right pressed for action on a voting bill championed by President Trump.

title: Tom Kean, New Jersey Congressman, Says Depression Led to Long Absence
summary: After months of mystery, the New Jersey representative broke his silence about the undisclosed health issue that prompted his 117-day absence.

title: What Tom Kean Did and Didn’t Explain About His Absence
summary: Representative Thomas Kean Jr., a New Jersey Republican, has finally reappeared in Congress. Questions remain about his nearly four-month absence from public life, which he said was due to depression.

title: For Many Americans, Depression Is Familiar. Here’s What We Know.
summary: Representative Thomas Kean Jr. announced he had been hospitalized for depression. More than one in four U.S. adults report having been diagnosed with the condition.

title: Talarico Seizes on Paxton Tabloid Story to Test Corruption Attacks in Texas Senate Race
summary: After a salacious report about Ken Paxton, the Republican nominee for Senate in Texas, his Democratic rival, James Talarico, seized on the news — but focused on corruption and affordability.

title: What to Watch in Colorado Primary Elections: Will Left-Wing Energy Keep Rising?
summary: Primaries on Tuesday will be the latest test of Democrats’ anti-establishment mood.

title: Trump Announces Republican Midterm Convention in Dallas, Texas
summary: Parties normally hold conventions only in presidential years, but President Trump liked the idea of mobilizing Republicans this fall.

title: Texas Is a Tossup. The Times/Siena Poll Points to How It Got There.
summary: A major shift among Hispanic voters and a favorable candidate matchup have helped put Democrats on the doorstep of a Senate upset.

title: Stocks Notch Strongest Quarter Since 2020
summary: The S&P 500 rose almost 15 percent for the three months through June, and many stock analysts remain optimistic that corporate earnings driven by artificial intelligence will keep growing.

title: Record-Breaking Heat Wave Expected to Hit New York City Ahead of July 4 Weekend
summary: Mayor Zohran Mamdani signed an executive order that aims to protect people who work outdoors. Temperatures this week could exceed 100 degrees.

title: After a Tap Heard in Rubble, U.S. Rescue Teams Begin a Grueling Marathon
summary: Surrounded by the devastation of Venezuela’s earthquakes, emergency specialists from California, Virginia and Florida work with locals to search for survivors.

title: Trump Officials Sideline Machado, Venezuela’s Opposition Leader, Over Earthquake Response
summary: U.S. officials called a bid by María Corina Machado, the Nobel Peace Prize winner, to return to earthquake-battered Venezuela a “political stunt” that has distracted from recovery efforts.

title: Aid Workers in Venezuela Fear Disease Outbreaks After Twin Quakes
summary: Health care workers worry that illnesses like cholera could spread in areas where clean water was already in short supply, and last week’s earthquakes destroyed water systems.

title: The Prom Went On in Kyiv, but Masha’s Date Danced Alone
summary: Masha Polska, 15, was an avid dancer who had been dreaming of a star turn in the group waltz. That was not to be.

title: The Dress Code for Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s Wedding? Expect a Black-Tie Event.
summary: New details have emerged about the celebration at Madison Square Garden, widely believed to be a wedding, including a dress code.

title: Rays of Hope in Dark Times
summary: It’s time to update Learned Hand’s great “Spirit of Liberty” address.

title: The Supreme Court Gets Birthright Citizenship Right
summary: The Constitution is clear. People who are born in this country and subject to its laws are citizens.

title: During Traffic Stop, N.F.L. Reporter Appeared to Show Off Her Access
summary: A body-worn camera recording shows Dianna Russini, formerly an “insider” for The Athletic, talking football with an officer and avoiding a citation.
